question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of IP-based or account-based rate limiting on getAirdrops() to perform high-frequency enumeration of all historical airdrop records across all accounts in the Hashgraph mirror database?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdParameter's support for multiple ID formats (num, shard.realm.num, alias, EVM address) to cause entityService.lookup() to resolve the same logical account to different numeric IDs depending on format, causing inconsistent historical views of Hashgraph airdrop records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a state-change validation in getAirdrops() to query historical airdrop records that reflect a pre-cancellation state, presenting a false view of active airdrops that were actually cancelled in Hashgraph history?
